description: One of the world's most dramatic coastlines — clifftop villages, pastel-coloured houses tumbling into turquoise water, limoncello at every turn, and a pace of life that feels like a film set.

neighborhoodRecs: Positano — The most iconic village, steep stairs, boutique hotels, best photography. Stay here for the classic experience.
Ravello — Hilltop town above the chaos, quieter, gardens, music festival in summer, stunning panoramas.
Praiano — Less touristy than Positano, local feel, own small beach, access to Furore fjord.
Atrani — Tiny village next to Amalfi town, fewer tourists, charming piazza, authentic southern Italian life.
areasToAvoid: Amalfi town main beach in August — uncomfortably packed, overpriced sunbeds, no real sand.
Driving the Amalfi Drive yourself in July/August — roads are gridlocked, buses can't pass, rental cars get scratched.
Budget accommodation near the main road — bus and scooter noise is relentless, earplugs essential.
scamWarnings: Taxi flat rates aren't displayed — always agree on the price before getting in. Official rates exist but drivers frequently ignore them.
Lemon products at touristy shops are often not from the Amalfi Coast at all. Buy limoncello from producers who show their orchard credentials.
Ferry touts at the dock sell unofficial tickets for inflated prices. Buy directly from the official booth — Travelmar and Metro del Mare are the legitimate operators.
Boat trip 'deals' from beach hawkers often cut corners on safety and don't include the caves they advertise. Use licensed operators.

airportTransfer.cheapest: SITA bus from Naples (Piazza Garibaldi) to Amalfi town via Salerno — €4 one way, takes 2.5–3 hours total from airport.
airportTransfer.fastest: Private transfer Naples Airport to Positano — €120–160, door to door, 1.5 hours.
airportTransfer.recommended: Take the Alibus airport shuttle to Naples city (€5), then Circumvesuviana train to Sorrento (€2.80, 50 min), then ferry to Positano in season (€15, 35 min). Best balance of cost and scenery.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Official taxi from Naples Airport to Positano: €130–180. Always negotiate or confirm fixed rate before departing.

tippingEtiquette: Italy has no strong tipping culture. Rounding up the bill or leaving €1–2 at restaurants is appreciated but not expected.
Coperto (cover charge) is legal and common — €2–5 per person added to your bill. Not a scam, just Italian custom.
Do not tip at bars or for counter service (standing at the bar costs less than sitting, by Italian tradition).
Dress modestly when visiting churches in Ravello and Amalfi — shoulders and knees covered.
Greet with 'buongiorno' (morning) and 'buonasera' (afternoon/evening) — jumping straight to your order without greeting is considered rude.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Budget guesthouse €90–120 | Mid-range hotel €180–280 | Boutique hotel €350–600+
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Seaside café lunch €14–20 | Dinner with wine €35–60 | Fine dining €80+
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: Ferry Positano–Amalfi €8 | SITA bus €2 | Private boat hire €150–300/half day
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Cave of the Emerald entry €5 | Sentiero degli Dei hike free | Cooking class €90–120 | Boat tour €40–60

packingTips: Walking sandals with ankle support — the paths are steep cobblestone and ordinary flip flops won't cut it.
Light layers even in summer — sea breeze drops temps 8–10°C in the evening.
Waterproof bag for boat trips — spray from the ferries is relentless.
Sunscreen factor 50+ — the sun reflects off the water intensely, even at 10am.
Small daypack — you'll be climbing stairs constantly, no place for wheeled luggage.
premium.bookingStrategy: Book May or September — warm, sunny, crowds at 60% of peak, prices 30–40% lower. July–August is peak season with queues everywhere and rooms booked a year in advance. Most places close Nov–Feb.
localTips: Take the Sentiero degli Dei (Path of the Gods) trail early morning — starts in Agerola, ends in Positano, 2.5 hours, jaw-dropping views with almost no one there before 9am.
The SITA bus is how locals get around — buy return tickets at the tobacconist (tabaccaio) to save queuing.
Fiorduomo restaurant in Amalfi town for honest pricing and great pasta — ignore the overpriced waterfront places.
Ravello's Villa Rufolo gardens at sunset is one of the best views in Italy and costs just €7.
Book ferry tickets in advance for peak weekends — they sell out.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: Mid-May to mid-June or September — perfect weather (22–26°C), manageable crowds, hotels available without a year's notice.
dayTrips: Capri — 30-min hydrofoil from Positano, stunning Blue Grotto, glamorous marina, day trip paradise.
Pompeii — 45 min by SITA bus from Sorrento, extraordinary Roman ruins, fascinating half-day.
Paestum — Ancient Greek temples an hour south, often overlooked, strikingly well-preserved.
visaInfo: EU/EEA citizens: no visa required — Italy is a full Schengen member with free movement for EU passport holders
UK citizens (post-Brexit): visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period; passport must be valid for the duration of stay
US, Canadian, and Australian citizens: visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period; no tourist visa required
Most other nationalities: consult the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs or nearest Italian consulate; many nationalities require a Schengen visa in advance
The 90-day Schengen allowance is shared across all 27 Schengen member states — time in any other Schengen country counts against the same quota
currencyTips: Currency: Euro (€) — Italy is a eurozone member; no currency exchange needed if arriving from another eurozone country
Cards are accepted at most hotels and restaurants along the Amalfi Coast, but smaller villages and local shops often prefer cash
Best ATMs: use bank-branded ATMs in Amalfi town or Sorrento — ATMs in smaller villages are scarce; withdraw cash before heading to Positano or Praiano
Avoid: dynamic currency conversion (DCC) at card terminals — always choose to pay in euros
Cash is essential for ferry tickets, SITA buses, small trattorias, and market stalls; carry €20–€50 in smaller notes
premium.simCard: TIM, Vodafone Italy, WindTre, and Iliad are the main Italian carriers. Coverage can be patchy on cliffside roads and in some villages — TIM generally has the best rural coverage. Buy a tourist SIM (€10–25, 10–20 GB with EU roaming) at Naples Airport (NAP) or in Sorrento. For pre-arrival setup, Airalo offers Italy eSIMs with instant activation.
